post.description:
This endpoint allows you to manually trigger a recovery email for an abandoned cart specified by its ID. It checks the cart's status, and if it hasn't been recovered, it will regenerate the recovery URL using the Blink checkout session and dispatch a recovery email to the customer. It increments the recovery email sent counter regardless of email success.

**Usage:** Useful when you want to manually re-initiate the recovery process for a specific cart.

get.description:
Retrieves details of a specific abandoned cart using its ID. The response includes information about the cart, such as customer email, status, and product details.

**Usage:** Use this endpoint to view details of a forgotten cart, aiding in customer follow-up or analytics.

Lists all abandoned carts for a store with optional filtering by cart status or product ID. Supports pagination.

**Usage:** Retrieve a list of all forgotten carts for a store. This can help in analyzing trends and planning follow-up strategies.

post.description:
Manually sends a recovery email for a specified abandoned cart. This endpoint can be used to re-initiate customer communication for carts awaiting recovery.

**Usage:** Directly reach out to customers who have left items in their carts, potentially increasing order completion rates.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of orders that are attributed to a specific affiliate based on their membership ID. Provides detailed order information, including the status, amount, and commission.

**Usage:** Allows affiliates to track their performance and commissions by viewing the orders they have influenced.

Retrieves details of a specific affiliate membership using its ID. It includes affiliate profile information and conversion statistics.

**Usage:** Use this to get detailed insights into an individual affiliate's performance, including total revenue and pending commissions.

patch.description:
Updates the details of a specific affiliate membership using its ID. You can change the status of the affiliation.

**Usage:** Utilize this endpoint to modify affiliate statuses like changing them to 'approved', 'banned', etc.

post.description:
Programmatically sends an email invitation to a potential affiliate. The email contains a link for joining the affiliate program.

**Usage:** Useful for programatically adding affiliates to expand marketing reach.

Generates a custom tracking link for an affiliate membership using a specific code.

**Usage:** Allows affiliates to create personalized links that can track their referral traffic and conversions.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of affiliates for a store, with optional filtering by status and pagination support.

**Usage:** Analyze and manage affiliates for promotional strategies.

post.description:
Approves, rejects, or bans an affiliate's application based on the action specified.

**Usage:** Control the participation of affiliates based on their performance or issues.

get.description:
Fetches the details of a specific audience member using their ID. This includes personal information and order history.

**Usage:** Utilize this endpoint to get deeper insights into specific customer profiles for personalized marketing strategies.

patch.description:
Updates the details of a specific audience member using their ID. This can include changes in name or subscription status.

**Usage:** Update customer data for better engagement and communication strategies.

delete.description:
Marks an audience member as unsubscribed without deleting their order history.

**Usage:** Softly remove customers who wish to opt-out without losing their historical data.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of promotional bumps available in the store. A bump is a special offer or upsell related to a product. The response includes details like the title, description, price, and deliverables.

**Usage:** Utilize this endpoint to analyze or display product upsells and promotions for increasing average order value.

post.description:
Creates a new promotional bump for the store. Includes details like title, description, and price.

**Usage:** Use this endpoint to create new upsells and promotional offers to increase average order value.

get.description:
Fetches the status and details of a specific Blink checkout session using its ID. The session details include URL, status, payment status, customer email, etc.

**Usage:** Use this endpoint to review the status of a checkout session and get necessary details for follow-up actions.

Retrieves a list of checkout sessions for a store with optional filtering by status. Supports pagination.

**Usage:** Analyze all checkout sessions to monitor activities and trends in your store.

post.description:
Initializes a new Blink checkout session for a product and returns the session URL. Can be customized with discounts, affiliate codes, and display preferences.

**Usage:** This endpoint is crucial for starting a payment flow and redirecting customers to a payment page.

get.description:
Provides the balance of credits for each meter associated with a customer. Meters track specific activities or resources, and this endpoint shows how much credit remains.

**Usage:** Useful for analyzing customer usage and planning billing adjustments, if applicable.

Retrieves a list of orders made by a specific customer. Includes information such as order status, amount, and product details.

**Usage:** Use this endpoint to manage customer relationships and analyze buying patterns.

post.description: Generates a 15-minute magic link that logs the customer into their order portal. Use this endpoint to provide customers with quick access to view and manage their orders.

get.description: Fetches full details of a specific customer, including name, email, status, total orders, and lifetime revenue.

patch.description: Updates a customer's details such as name, email, or status. Use this endpoint to modify customer records in the database.

get.description: Retrieves all active subscriptions for a given customer. Useful for determining current and past engagements with customers.
Fetches a list of customers for a store with optional filters by email and status. Supports pagination to handle large data sets effectively.

post.description: Manually creates a new customer record in the store, useful for customer onboarding and creating customer profiles manually.

get.description: Fetches details of a specific discount by its ID including code, type, and associated products.

patch.description: Updates information for a specific discount, such as code, amount, or expiration date. Use to edit existing discount offers.

delete.description: Deletes a discount from the database. This is irreversible and should be used when the discount is no longer needed.

get.description: Retrieves a list of discounts for a store with optional filtering by status. Supports pagination.

post.description: Creates a new discount for the store. Ensures unique discount code within the store.

get.description: Fetches a list of disputes associated with the store's connected Blink account. Supports limiting results.

post.description: Sends a targeted email to a segment of customers filtered by tag or all.
Logs a custom event for a store using Umami analytics. Tracks user activities, marketing campaigns, or other events.

Permanently disable a license key to prevent further use. Requires `x-store-id` for verification and checks the key’s status before revocation. Suitable when a license has been compromised or needs termination.

get.description: Fetch complete details of a license key using its ID, including status, activations, product, and customer information. Requires `x-store-id` for authentication and confirmation of ownership.

patch.description: Allows updating of specific fields in a license key such as expiration date, activation limit, or status. Requires `x-store-id` for validation.

post.description: Activates a license key by incrementing its activation count, performing validation checks for key status, expiration, and activation limits. Requires `x-store-id` and `license_key` in the request body.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of license keys for the store with optional filtering by status, product ID, or order ID. Supports pagination.

**Usage:** Useful for managing license keys issued to customers, including monitoring their status and usage.

post.description:
Validates a license key's status, expiration, and activation limits.

**Usage:** Ensures license keys are active and have not reached their usage or expiration limits before they are used.

get.description:
Fetches specific details of a meter identified by its ID.

**Usage:** Allows store owners to view and manage the measurement metrics tied to particular activities or resources within their store.

patch.description:
Updates the details of a specific meter, such as name or unit.

**Usage:** Modify meter configurations to ensure accurate tracking or reporting of metrics.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of all meters within a store, with options for pagination.

**Usage:** Provides a comprehensive view of all meters being tracked, helping in resource management and strategy planning.

post.description:
Adds a new meter for tracking a specific event or resource.

**Usage:** Essential for tracking new metrics in order to better understand usage and performance trends within the store.

get.summary: Retrieve metrics for revenue, orders, and subscriptions
get.description:
Provides statistics for revenue, orders, subscriptions, and customer counts within a given date range.

**Usage:** Use to derive insights into sales performance and customer growth over times specific periods.

Fetches a list of organizations associated with the store identified by `x-store-id`. Supports pagination with `limit` and `page` query parameters. Each organization includes details such as email, organization name, and billing email.

**Usage:** Useful for managing and analyzing organizations linked to your store.

post.description:
Creates a new organization within the store using `x-store-id`. Requires `email` and `organization_name`. Optionally accepts `billing_email`, defaulting to the provided `email` if not supplied.

**Usage:** Useful for adding new organizational entities to be associated with your store.

get.description:
Fetches a list of Blink payouts for the store identified by `x-store-id`. Requires a connected Blink account. Supports limiting the result set.

**Usage:** Useful for financial reconciliation and tracking payouts from Blink.

Fetches detailed information about a product, including variants and deliverables, for a given product ID. Requires `x-store-id`.

**Usage:** Provides comprehensive insights into a product's configuration, variants, and linked digital deliverables for store management and product cataloging.

**Path

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID or the human-readable slug of the product

patch.description:
Updates an existing product's attributes such as name, price, or status. A product ID is required. Requires `x-store-id`.

**Usage:** Suitable for modifying product listings directly in the store's catalog, allowing for dynamically adjusting product offerings.

**Path

delete.description:
Archives a product by setting its status to 'archived'. This action does not delete the product but prevents it from being sold. Requires `x-store-id`.

**Usage:** Utilized when a product should be temporarily or permanently withdrawn from active sale while keeping its configuration for historical or analytical purposes.

**Path

get.description:
Retrieve a list of all variants associated with a specific product. Requires `x-store-id`.

**Usage:** Useful for detailed inventory management and displaying all available options for a product.

**Path

post.description:
Adds a new variant to a product. Requires `name` and `price`, along with other optional parameters. Requires `x-store-id`.

**Usage:** Valuable for expanding a product's options, such as adding new sizes or colors to a clothing product.

**Path

get.description:
Fetches detailed information about a specific product variant. Requires `x-store-id`.

**Usage:** Provides comprehensive insights into a variant's configuration for inventory management and display purposes.

**Path

patch.description:
Updates attributes for a specific product variant, such as name, price, or status. Requires `x-store-id`.

**Usage:** Useful for maintaining variant accuracy and availability within the store catalog.

**Path

delete.description:
Deletes a specific product variant. This action is irreversible. Requires `x-store-id`.

**Usage:** Suitable for removing specific variant options from a product's offering.

**Path

get.description:
Retrieves a list of all products associated with a specific store. Supports pagination and filtering by status and pricing model. This endpoint provides detailed product information including variants and images.

**Usage:** Useful for managing the store's product catalog and reviewing available inventory.

post.description:
Creates a new product in the store. Requires detailed product information including name, pricing model, and price. Optionally includes settings for subscription intervals, trial days, and license key configurations.

**Usage:** Adds new products to the store's offerings, supporting different pricing models and configurations.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of refunds associated with a store's connected Blink account. Supports limiting the results.

**Usage:** Useful for financial reconciliation and tracking refund activities within the store.

Fetches a list of subscription invoices for the store. Supports filtering by status, subscription ID, and customer ID. Supports pagination.

**Usage:** Useful for tracking and managing subscription invoices and financial records.

Fetches detailed information about a specific subscription. Includes product and variant details associated with the subscription.

**Usage:** Useful for understanding customer subscriptions and managing their lifecycle.

**Path

patch.description:
Updates the status of a subscription, allowing actions such as cancelation, immediate cancelation, or resumption.

**Usage:** Useful for managing the lifecycle of subscriptions, including cancelation and resumption.

**Path

get.description:
Retrieves a list of all subscriptions for a given store. Supports filtering by status, customer ID, and product ID. Includes pagination.

**Usage:** Useful for managing and analyzing all active and past subscriptions.

post.description: Programmatically migrate external license keys into Blink.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of revenue splits for the store.

post.description: Creates a new revenue split and automatically invites the recipients.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing revenue split.

patch.description: Updates the specified split. When updating the recipients or products array, the entire array is replaced with the newly provided one.

get.description:
Fetches a paginated list of all orders.

**Usage:** Build a custom seller dashboard or sync orders to an external ERP/CRM system automatically.
Fetches detailed information for a specific order, including customer info and paid amounts.

post.description:
Programmatically issue a full or partial refund for an order.

**Usage:** Headless event organizers managing bulk ticket cancellations directly from their custom admin panel without logging into Blink.

amount.description: Amount in cents to refund (optional, defaults to full)

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all invoices generated by subscriptions or manual billing.
Retrieves details of a specific invoice.

Generates and downloads a secure PDF receipt of the invoice.

**Usage:** Enterprise agencies building custom portals where B2B clients can download their tax-compliant PDF receipts on demand.

Fetches orders securely for a specific customer authenticated via portal magic link.

**Usage:** Headless apps building completely custom 'My Account' or 'Order History' pages for their buyers.

post.description:
Generates a secure, temporary URL to the Blink Customer Billing Portal.

**Usage:** SaaS companies allowing their users to update credit cards or cancel subscriptions directly from the company's own custom settings page.

get.description: Fetches active and inactive subscriptions securely for an authenticated customer.

post.description:
Calculates precise digital tax rates based on the buyer's IP and billing address before charging them.

**Usage:** Hybrid hardware/software companies building 100% custom checkout flows that need to show real-time tax line items to the user before they click 'Pay'.
Validates an EU VAT or international tax ID via the VIES network to automatically apply reverse-charge exemptions.

get.description: Lists reusable checkout links.

post.description:
Creates a permanent checkout link.

**Usage:** Automatically generating short-links for influencer marketing campaigns.

get.description: Lists all configured webhook endpoints.

post.description:
Programmatically registers a new webhook endpoint to listen to events like `order.created` or `subscription.updated`.

**Usage:** DevTools automatically registering their syncing endpoints when a user installs their integration.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of all newsletter subscribers and customers in your audience.

**Usage:** Media brands exporting their audience list to a custom analytics warehouse.

post.description:
Triggers a mass email broadcast to your audience via Resend.

**Usage:** Newsletters using a headless CMS to automatically blast out their latest article to all paid subscribers.
Creates a new tag that can be assigned to customers.

Extremely high-throughput endpoint to inject usage events.

**Usage:** API-first dev tools reporting token consumption every time their customer makes a request, allowing Blink to calculate the exact invoice at the end of the month.

get.description:
Fetches the current real-time prepaid credit balance for a customer.

**Usage:** AI tools displaying 'Credits Remaining: 500' in their app header.
Lists purchasing power parity (PPP) and strict geo-pricing rules.

post.description:
Automatically sets up localized pricing overrides for specific countries.

**Usage:** Global gaming studios deploying a script to adjust virtual currency prices dynamically across 50+ countries based on local inflation.

Calculates the exact proration amounts before upgrading or downgrading a subscription.

**Usage:** E-Learning apps letting users preview exactly how much they will be charged (or credited) today if they upgrade from the 'Basic' tier to 'Pro Max'.

get.description: Retrieve a list of files (deliverables) associated with a store, filtered optionally by product or variant ID. The endpoint supports pagination and requires a valid `x-store-id` in the headers for authentication.

Fetch detailed information about a file by its ID. This endpoint requires the `x-store-id` in the headers to verify ownership. Successfully retrieves information such as file title, description, URL, and creation date. Useful for accessing and managing deliverables associated with specific products or variants.

delete.description: Deletes a file by its ID after ensuring ownership via `x-store-id`. This action is irreversible and should be used with caution. It checks if the file exists and belongs to the correct store before deletion.

get.description: Generates a time-limited pre-signed URL for downloading a specific file.

get.summary: List all deliverables for an order
get.description:
Retrieves the complete fulfillment payload for a specific order. This is the ultimate fulfillment endpoint for custom storefronts. It automatically generates 1-hour secure pre-signed URLs for any file downloads, fetches any License Keys auto-generated during checkout, and returns dynamic claim URLs for Discord integrations.

**Usage:** A company building a custom 'My Library' or 'All Access' dashboard where buyers log in to access their purchased goods securely.

200.description: List of deliverables unlocked by this order

post.description:
Programmatically triggers Blink's automatic fulfillment email to the buyer of this order. This email contains their secure download links, license keys, and order receipt.

**Usage:** A seller with a custom support dashboard wants to build a one-click 'Resend Receipt' button for their customer support team when a buyer says they didn't receive their email.

get.description:
Retrieves the complete fulfillment payload for an order, authenticated via the Customer Portal JWT. This allows buyers to securely access their downloads, license keys, and Discord roles directly from the frontend without routing through your custom backend.

**Usage:** You are rendering a 'My Purchases' page in React and want to fetch the buyer's files directly from the browser using their session token.

200.description: List of deliverables unlocked by this order

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing payout.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the payout to retrieve.

success_url.description: The URL to redirect the customer to upon a successful payment. If provided, Blink will immediately redirect the iframe/browser to this URL instead of showing the default success message and deliverables. (Use this for custom fulfillment or routing).

layout.description: The layout template to use for this checkout. If you intend to use the overlay script (`embed.js`), you **must** set this to `overlay` to return the correct URL parameters. Otherwise the overlay script will fail.

custom_checkout_id.description: UUID of the custom checkout design

tags.name: Splits
tags.description: Manage revenue splits and automatic payouts
